content.value: Everywhere else in this walkthrough, Eq. 10 is kept in the paper's compact order. Under that same convention, the bounds minus pi over 2 to pi over 2 are the old angle bounds carried by the pre-collapse expression.
That is why the shortcut below is inconsistent. Theta has already been integrated out, but the line keeps d omega while reusing the old angle bounds minus pi over 2 to pi over 2.

So the issue is not just that the variable is omega. The issue is that the line mixes the surviving measure d omega with bounds that still belong to the pre-collapse angle window. After the collapse, the remaining one-variable integral must use the locked omega-domain instead.
